title: Melty Indulgent Mexican Polish Sausage Carnitas
description: A rich and flavorful Mexican dish featuring Polish sausage, melted cheese, and crispy fried elements, served in a traditional kazan cauldron, inspired by the bold flavors of Chiapas, and made with ayurvedic rajasic friendly ingredients to promote energy and vitality.

ingredients.name: Polish sausage
ingredients.notes: preferably with a high umami content

ingredients.name: Mexican cheese blend
ingredients.notes: such as Oaxaca or Chihuahua cheese

instructions: Heat the lard or oil in a large kazan cauldron over medium heat.
Add the sliced onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the Polish sausage and cook until browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the oregano and cumin, and stir to combine.
Using a slotted spoon, remove the sausage from the cauldron and set it aside.
Add more lard or oil if necessary, and then add the tortillas, one at a time, and fry until crispy and golden, about 30 seconds per side.
To assemble the carnitas, place a portion of the fried tortillas on a plate, then add a few slices of the Polish sausage, some of the caramelized onion, and a sprinkle of the Mexican cheese blend.
Place the plate under the broiler and cook until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove from the oven and top with a dollop of sour cream, some chopped cilantro, and serve immediately.
